steps.prompt: Which word is the finite verb?

hints.panem: 'Panem' is a noun meaning 'bread' in the accusative
hints.regno: 'Regno' is a noun meaning 'kingdom' in the ablative

hints.present indicative: Present would be 'manducat' — 'manducabit' has the future -bi- marker
hints.future perfect indicative: Future perfect would be 'manducaverit' — 'manducabit' is simple future

hints.mando, mandare, mandavi, mandatum: These are the parts of 'mandare' (to entrust/command)
hints.edo, edere, edi, esum: These are the parts of 'edere' (to eat) — classical synonym but different verb
hints.mando, mandere, mandi, mansum: These are the parts of 'mandere' (to chew) — related but distinct from 'manducare'

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'panis' — the -em ending marks accusative
hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'pane' — 'panem' with -em is accusative
hints.dative: Dative would be 'pani' — 'panem' is accusative, direct object of 'manducabit'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'panis' has gen. in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'panis' has gen. in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'panis' has gen. in -is

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'regnum' — 'regno' follows 'in' indicating place
hints.dative: 'Regno' could be dative, but after 'in' (place where) it is ablative

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'Deus' — 'Dei' is genitive singular
hints.dative: Dative would be 'Deo' — 'Dei' ends in -i marking genitive
hints.vocative: Vocative would be 'Deus' or 'Dee' — 'Dei' is genitive

steps.prompt: What is the function of the 'qui' clause?
steps.target: relative clause modifying an implied subject
choices: relative clause modifying an implied subject

hints.indirect question: An indirect question would use subjunctive — 'manducabit' is indicative
hints.purpose clause: A purpose clause uses 'ut/ne' + subjunctive, not 'qui' + indicative
hints.result clause: A result clause uses 'ut' + subjunctive — this is a simple relative clause

translation: Blessed is he who will eat bread in the kingdom of God
